strengths: Active TypeScript/Node.js production experience at Optilyz and re:cinq covers the core backend requirement
Current role includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, directly matching the AI agent development requirement
Long career spanning embedded, mobile, web, and systems work demonstrates genuine full-stack breadth
concerns: Nest.js — the primary backend framework — appears nowhere in the resume; the candidate's Node.js work shows raw/custom or GraphQL patterns, not the structured, decorator-driven architecture this role demands

React exposure is thin and undifferentiated (one brief mention at Optilyz); the frontend portfolio is dominated by Ember.js, signalling the candidate's default frontend instinct is misaligned with the required stack
Kotlin is entirely absent; Java surfaces only in peripheral robotics work at Altom, leaving the secondary-language requirement essentially unmet and raising doubt about JVM backend credibility
response.justification: The candidate has genuine seniority and a breadth of systems exposure, but the resume does not demonstrate proficiency in three of the role's most specific technical requirements: Nest.js (the primary backend framework), React at any meaningful depth (Ember.js is the consistent frontend choice throughout the career), and Kotlin (not mentioned at all). The testing-framework requirement — Vitest, Jest, Playwright — is also unaddressed; the only testing tooling highlighted is bespoke D-language libraries. With the primary stack alignment this weak, the candidate would face a steep onboarding ramp on the frameworks the team ships daily, making them a risky choice against candidates who already work inside this exact technology surface.
